steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the function of the underlined sentence: ‘In 2013 Bridie J.M. Allan and colleagues published a study concluding that ocean acidification has a strong effect on the behavior of Pomacentrus amboinensis.’ The word ‘concluding’ is the key — it signals that what follows is the study’s result, not a critique, a list of traits, or a location.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘It presents a criticism’: The underlined sentence simply reports what Allan and colleagues found. The criticism (small sample size, potential bias) comes in the sentences that follow, attributed to Clark and colleagues — not to Allan. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘It lists several traits of Pomacentrus amboinensis’: The sentence never describes traits of the fish. It only names the species as the subject of the study. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘It emphasizes a detail about where … the study was conducted’: No location is mentioned anywhere in the underlined sentence. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘It states the conclusion reached by Allan and colleagues in their 2013 study’: The sentence explicitly says Allan and colleagues published a study ‘concluding that’ ocean acidification has a strong effect — stating their conclusion directly. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
